concerns.text: Unresolved threshold conflict: SC-003 specifies Monte Carlo validation threshold ≤0.01, SC-026 specifies ≤0.005, FR-026 states ≤0.005. These conflicting thresholds create ambiguity about which success criterion governs verification. This was flagged in the kickback and remains unresolved.

FR-002 references external files 'data-model.md' and 'extracted_summary.schema.yaml' not included in the spec. Cross-references should either be self-contained or clearly indicate external dependencies. This was flagged in the kickback and remains unresolved.

FR-030 cites 'Kohavi et al. (2020)' but lacks full bibliographic details. Constitution Principle II requires verified citations with title-token-overlap ≥0.7. This was flagged in the kickback and remains unresolved.

SC-003 and SC-026 both reference Monte Carlo validation but with different thresholds (0.01 vs 0.005). SC-026 references FR-026 but does not resolve the threshold conflict. This creates an untestable contradiction.

FR-026 mandates Monte Carlo validation with 10,000 replicates, but SC-003 uses a different threshold (0.01 vs 0.005). The spec does not clarify which threshold governs verification, leaving the success criterion ambiguous.
